quiz1.missionText: Like the cat photo on your timeline

insight.title: Why could you like with a double tap?
insight.subtitle: Double tap = like is the standard SNS gesture
insight.doubleTapTitle: Double tap is a shortcut for liking
insight.doubleTapDesc: On X (formerly Twitter) and Instagram, quickly tapping a photo twice gives it a like. No need to find the heart button — it's an intuitive shortcut.
insight.heartTitle: The heart animation confirms your action
insight.heartDesc: When you double tap, a large heart appears in the center of the image. This is called feedback animation — it lets you know the app recognized your gesture.
insight.gestureTitle: SNS gestures are universal across apps
insight.gestureDesc: Double tap to like is a common gesture across Instagram, X, TikTok, and many other social apps. Learn it once and use it everywhere.

quiz2.missionText: Close the fullscreen image and return to the timeline

insight.title: Why could you close by swiping down?
insight.subtitle: Swipe down = close/dismiss is an intuitive gesture
insight.swipeTitle: Swipe down is the intuitive gesture to close
insight.swipeDesc: Swiping a fullscreen image downward closes it and returns to the previous screen. The gravity-like 'drop' motion intuitively maps to dismissing content.
insight.fullscreenTitle: Fullscreen mode opens with a tap
insight.fullscreenDesc: In SNS apps, tapping a post image opens it in fullscreen mode, allowing you to pinch to zoom and view it in detail.
insight.backTitle: You can close without an X button
insight.backDesc: On smartphones, swipe gestures let you close screens even without a visible X button. Trying gestures even without obvious cues is key.

quiz3.missionText: Scrolled too far! Jump back to the top instantly

insight.title: How did you jump back to the top so fast?
insight.subtitle: Tap status bar or home tab = scroll to top
insight.statusBarTitle: Tapping the status bar scrolls to the top
insight.statusBarDesc: Tapping the status bar (the clock area on iPhone) instantly scrolls to the top in many apps. It saves you from manually scrolling back up through a long feed.
insight.homeTabTitle: Tapping the active home tab also scrolls to top
insight.homeTabDesc: Tapping the currently active tab (like the Home button) a second time scrolls the timeline back to the top — a trick that works in many apps beyond just SNS.
insight.efficientTitle: Jumping is far faster than scrolling
insight.efficientDesc: Manually scrolling back to the top through endless content is inefficient. Knowing the shortcut gesture makes app navigation dramatically faster.

quiz4.missionText: Switch to your secret sub account

insight.title: Why could you switch accounts with a long press?
insight.subtitle: Long press the profile icon to switch accounts
insight.longPressTitle: Long press the profile icon to choose accounts
insight.longPressDesc: In many SNS apps, long pressing the profile icon or avatar reveals an account-switching menu. It lets you quickly switch between multiple accounts.
insight.subAccountTitle: Sub accounts are kept separate from main accounts
insight.subAccountDesc: Many SNS users maintain a main account and a sub account for hobbies or anonymous posting. The long press shortcut makes switching between them quick and easy.
insight.multiAccountTitle: Managing multiple accounts is a modern skill
insight.multiAccountDesc: Having multiple SNS accounts — for work, personal use, hobbies — has become commonplace. Mastering account switching helps you use SNS more effectively.
